rows.spec: S5.2: assignments with no argv produce no command and no refusal.

S5.2 (amended 2026-07-21 by owner ratification during the Tier 3B predeclaration adjudication): assignments plus argv where every assignment has a statically known name and a fully literal value (value-known true for all) certify with the resolved argv and no refusal; a literal prefix such as PATH=x never hides the invocation, it is still reported.

S5.2 (amended 2026-07-21 by owner ratification during the Tier 3B predeclaration adjudication): assignments plus argv where any assignment value is dynamic or not statically known refuse assignment-prefix at the earliest such assignment while the certified argv is still resolved so a definite finding is retained.
S5.2: a first argv word with text=None refuses unstable-first-word with no string-based head logic.
S5.2: any word with single=False refuses the appropriate expansion or splitting refusal at that word.
S5.2: the IR invariant text is not None implies single is True is enforced at the boundary.
